props.title: Digest: AI agents that transact onchain

props.text: A $1.79T asset manager put agentic AI in writing as blockchain's "killer use case," projecting a $3-5T agentic-commerce market by 2030 — a quiet day otherwise.
Franklin Templeton calls agentic AI blockchain's "killer use case"

Franklin Templeton's digital-assets team argued this week that AI agents can't use card rails or bank accounts for sub-cent micropayments, so blockchain becomes the only infrastructure that works economically — and told institutional clients to buy the tokens of the networks powering it, projecting a $3-5 trillion agentic-commerce market by 2030.

Why it matters: A $1.79T traditional asset manager is now telling institutions to buy crypto specifically because AI agents need onchain payment rails — a credibility marker builders can cite that outranks the usual VC/founder hype.
"Sherwood" guardian-verified agent-fund pitch on Robinhood Chain couldn't be reconciled against a same-named site that reads as a meme token.
July x402 wash-trading study (independently-verified spend as low as ~$188K vs. a $24M headline) is real but 5 days old and its own reported figures disagree across sources.
WhisprVirtuals "private agent payments" teaser had zero independent corroboration.
